name: Denjang Jiege Keh (Spiced Crab Soup)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Soft bean curd; cut into 1/2-inch cubes
1 tsp Hot red chili powder; mixed with 1 tablespoon water
1 slice Ginger; about a quarter-sized piece
recipeInstructions:
The people of Kwangju, where this recipe originates, enjoy food that is salty and chili-hot. The fermented Denjang paste forms the base of the seasonings, complemented by the chili and ginger. The fresh crabs absorb the flavors, making the entire soup very appealing. One August, during a three-day pre-typhoon downpour, I visited my teacher's home as sheets of water poured from the sky on my taxi ride to town. Despite the intense rain over the rice fields, I was glad when I arrived and could focus on cooking the crabs and preparing the soup.
1. Place the water and Denjang paste in a pan; simmer over low heat with the lid covered for 10 minutes.
2. Add the bean curd cubes and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
3. Incorporate all other ingredients and continue cooking for another 15 minutes.
4. Serve the soup in four individual bowls, accompanied by rice and various side dishes.
